name: Sichuan Cashew Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice the chicken breasts into 1/2 inch pieces. Dip in the egg white, then in the cornstarch mixed with a pinch of salt.
2. Heat some oil until fairly hot and fry the diced chicken. Drain.
3. Slice the peppers in half vertically. Remove the seeds, stems, and pith. Cut into 1/2 inch squares.
4. Cut the parboiled bamboo shoots to the same size as the peppers. Mix the sauce ingredients together in a bowl.
5. Fry the cashew nuts in moderately hot oil until they are lightly browned and crunchy.
6. Heat 2 tbsp of oil in the wok and stir fry the garlic, bamboo shoots, and peppers. Stir fry briefly over high heat.
7. Add the chicken and cashew nuts, sprinkle with rice wine, and pour in the sauce. Stir fry all the ingredients together until heated through, adding a pinch of MSG (optional) to enhance the flavor.
